note: Market-research reference content for French SME IPO candidates: Euronext admission segments (Access, Access+, Growth, regulated) and per-sector market context + IPO-window notes. Admission thresholds are drawn from Euronext listing rules and the June 2026 EU Listing Act; sector context is curated from public secondary sources and is directional, not a live market feed. This is orientation for a first assessment, not investment advice or a guarantee of admission — final eligibility is set by Euronext and the AMF and should be confirmed with a listing sponsor.
listingActNote: Since 5 June 2026 the EU Listing Act applies: a standardised €12M threshold below which no prospectus is required, English as the conventional disclosure language, a standardised prospectus format, and (pending national transposition) a move toward a 10% free-float requirement. Source: Euronext, 'Euronext introduces a new admission framework' (June 2026).

segments.label: Euronext Access
segments.summary: Entry-level MTF for start-ups and SMEs joining a market to finance growth and gain visibility, with the simplest admission criteria.
segments.bestFor: Early-stage companies not yet meeting Growth criteria; admission possible via private placement or technical admission.
segments.source: Euronext, 'Choosing a Market' and Euronext Access Rule Book — no minimum public holding or market-cap requirement.

segments.label: Euronext Access+
segments.summary: A special compartment of Access tailored to fast-growing SMEs, acting as a springboard toward Growth and the regulated market.
segments.bestFor: Fast-growing SMEs wanting a step up from Access with more visibility and support.
segments.source: Euronext Access Rule Book — Access+ requires equity securities of at least €1M in public hands; two years of accounts expected.

segments.label: Euronext Growth
segments.summary: An SME growth market (MTF) with proportionate obligations — the typical destination for an established, profitable French SME.
segments.bestFor: Established SMEs with two years of audited accounts and enough size to sustain a liquid free float.
segments.source: CMS Expert Guide to International ECM Listings (France): Euronext Growth Paris requires a €2.5M minimum free float on a public offering and two years of audited annual accounts; the €10M cap figure is an indicative working floor for a natural fit, not a hard rule.

segments.label: Euronext (regulated market)
segments.summary: The main EU-regulated market, with the fullest disclosure and governance obligations and the deepest institutional investor access.
segments.bestFor: Larger, mature companies able to meet full regulated-market obligations and free-float expectations.
segments.source: Indicative working thresholds for a natural fit on the regulated market (typical ~€25M+ cap, higher free float); exact criteria set by Euronext/AMF.

software.sizeTrend: The French software/SaaS market has grown steadily on digitalisation and cloud adoption; recurring-revenue models command the highest valuation multiples of any SME sector.
software.ipoWindow: Historically the most active French SME-IPO sector; investor appetite is strong for profitable, recurring-revenue software but has become more selective on unprofitable growth since 2023.
software.source: Directional synthesis of Hectelion 2026 multiples commentary and general French tech-market coverage; not a quantified market-size study.

industrials.sizeTrend: A large, mature base of French industrial SMEs (ETI/PME); reshoring and energy-transition capex are supportive structural themes.
industrials.ipoWindow: Steadier, valuation-sensitive investor demand; capital-intensive profiles are judged on order-book visibility and margins rather than growth alone.
industrials.source: Directional synthesis of Argos Index mid-market commentary and general industrials coverage.

healthcare.sizeTrend: Medtech and healthcare services benefit from ageing demographics and innovation; biotech is a distinct, binary-risk profile.
healthcare.ipoWindow: Selective; profitable medtech/services attract steady interest, while pre-revenue biotech IPO windows open and close with risk appetite.
healthcare.source: Directional synthesis of Hectelion healthcare multiples and general life-sciences coverage.

consumer.sizeTrend: Consumer/retail spans strong French brand and luxury adjacencies to thin-margin distribution; e-commerce continues to reshape the mix.
consumer.ipoWindow: Cyclical and confidence-sensitive; brand strength and margin resilience matter most to IPO investors.
consumer.source: Directional; no dedicated French SME consumer market-size source used.

food.sizeTrend: A defensive, lower-growth sector with a deep French agroalimentaire base; stable cash flows over rapid expansion.
food.ipoWindow: Defensive appeal in uncertain markets, but modest growth caps valuations and IPO frequency.
food.source: Directional synthesis of Damodaran food-processing data and general sector coverage.

energy.sizeTrend: Cleantech and renewables benefit from strong policy tailwinds and energy-transition capital, though project economics are capital-intensive.
energy.ipoWindow: Thematically favoured by ESG-oriented capital, but sensitive to interest rates and subsidy regimes; a lower-confidence sector for benchmarking.
energy.source: Directional; no dedicated French SME energy market-size source used.

business-services.sizeTrend: An asset-light, fragmented sector with steady demand for outsourcing and specialised B2B services.
business-services.ipoWindow: Steady, margin-driven investor interest; scalability and client concentration are the key questions.
business-services.source: Directional; no dedicated French SME business-services market-size source used.

default.sizeTrend: The French mid-market (PME/ETI) is a broad, active base for private and public capital; conditions vary widely by sub-sector.
default.ipoWindow: The French SME-IPO window has been more selective since 2023, favouring profitable, well-governed issuers; the June 2026 EU Listing Act aims to make listing easier and cheaper for SMEs.
default.source: Directional synthesis; general French mid-market and Euronext coverage.
